content: PanelText is supposed to be UI, and LanguageConfig contains some UI text and... sometimes the programmers just put whatever they wanna add into LanguageConfig so it's a bit of a mess😹
This is another case of our combat designers not being very consistent, according to them, so to be safe English is just going with Chinese for now (having two separate terms for these two)

if you change it to <CardTip:NoRetain> it will indeed show as <:warn:1408311425952387122>NoRetain, as long as you don't change the <CardTip: part!
I kinda knew it but was not 100% sure. Thank you for confirming!
I understand you want to flavor up the text, but we will have to ask you to refrain from doing it now<:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541>
thread_title: On adding Flavor where the Text is otherwise Bland

content:
Introducing stylistic variations at this stage risks creating player confusion and inconsistencies, as Morimens' English translation just "isn't there" yet: there are still machine translations and various errors. Speaking from experience, other players would probably complain about the change: because we as devs would be unable to make sure your stylistlic upgrade is consistently applied to the entire game.

Please don't see this as a rejection of your ideas! We are absolutely taking note of your fantastic suggestion for more flavorful text. When our dedicated English localizers are onboarded, we will share this feedback with them for consideration during the final polishing phase.

Your work is incredibly valuable in getting us to that point. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation!
thread_title: On adding Flavor where the Text is otherwise Bland

content:
Hi everyone!

If anyone could explain the difference between those two to me, I'd be indebted to you. They're both translated to Void in English, and I'm unsure if one of these isn't perhaps a noun and the other an adjective. Or if the usage context is different. (Relevant for German because Void (noun) and Void (adjective) are different words)

Thank you in advance!
thread_title: Question on 虚无 vs. 空虚

content:
虚无 is "void" as in nihility. It's more of a high-level conceptual thing, so it's generally for stuff like the abyss, cosmic void, nothingness, etc
空虚 is "void" as in emptiness, hollowness. It's more of a low-level physical thing, so like hollow emotions, empty shell, etc
thread_title: Question on 虚无 vs. 空虚
Question on 虚无 vs. 空虚

content: Thank you, that helps a ton!
thread_title: Question on 虚无 vs. 空虚

Hey! Was starting to get to grips with testing / proofreading, and was wondering how I'd go about this in regards to UI text and whatnot (Incredibly rusty/not used to github)

For example, I noticed an inconsistency within the Manual menu in EN where it says 'one-click collection' instead of something such as 'claim all' (pic attached of issue), where would I submit this? 

Want to make sure I get things right first time /double check i'm not misreading <:murphy_cake:1408324295180030002> 
Cheers :)

UI bugs and interface issues (cut off text, text formatting/syntax issues, wrong fields being linked, etc, should be reported in the Language Testing Mastersheet with a corresponding screenshot: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WQpzrkFNluWgAkKHQTLB1wbmJQFHp-HsYZ_Hm9_e_Vc/edit?gid=0#gid=0

Changes to text itself should be done via pulling the relevant row(s) from the Source sheet, modifying **only** the English (or whichever relevant language is being translated into) field, and then uploading it as a .csv file via the a Github pull request: https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1402537664619479103/1407610133676032114

Feel free to ask if you have any questions or need help with the process <:casiah_cute:1408324282895040572>

for that particular term, the phrase "One-Click Collection" seems to be used for all instances of multi-reward claiming so I don't think we have any ways to trace which specific instance of it is tied to the Manual screen (but also, I think it would be fine to simply replace all instances of it with "Claim All" as "Claim All" is a much more natural and standard terminology compared to "One-Click Collection")

All good! I've added a simple overflow interface issue on the sheet, hopefully that's okay <:celeste_pray:1408324281624170566> 

Will let you know if there's any issue when I work on the source sheet
